name: Holland v. Florida
question: Can "gross negligence" by counsel that directly results in the late filing of a petition for habeas corpus relief qualify as a circumstance warranting equitable tolling?
description: A case in which the Court held that a state prisoner who has exhausted their appeals process in their state has one year to file a writ of habeas corpus, with certain exceptions (including attorney misconduct) that may allow the courts to relax that deadline.
